title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: root service capabilities or service-level models depending upward on sealed capability modules through module or binding aliases, provider code reaching current-main through governance module or binding imports instead of the neutral root dependency, module router bypass of module context, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, package or CLI-local provider-binding value imports outside the native-resource binding and provider-resource composition root, controller release/evidence mechanics outside resource bindings, a second production value consumer of the lifecycle service client, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: The root declares ready capabilities without importing the sealed governance, packaging, providers, releases, or vendors modules through direct paths or their transition bindings, and that sealed set grows monotonically as context-direction slices land. Governance owns current-main selection while providers consume only its neutral root observation contract. The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and derives provider-domain repositories under service middleware. The root composes module routers through impl.router. Runtime construction narrows through one exact CLI composition root, with package provider-transition values admitted at the native-resource binding and composition root while the CLI-local provider index flows only into the composition root.
remediate: Declare root capabilities and cross-module observation contracts under service/model, consume current-main from service/model/dependencies/current-main rather than governance modules or bindings, derive service-owned repositories and adapters under module provided context, project exact capabilities into each module from module.ts, implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep remaining ports protocol/type-only, construct the lifecycle client only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/client.ts, use type-only lifecycle and provider-binding dependencies elsewhere, import package provider-transition values only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/bindings/providers/native.ts and apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/providers/node-runtime.ts, import the CLI-local provider index only from that node-runtime composition root, and keep release/evidence mechanics behind resource contracts.
message: Lifecycle dependencies flow from root-owned capabilities and neutral cross-module observations into sealed module contexts and handlers, then through impl.router and one qualified CLI composition root with exact package and CLI-local provider binding edges.
